description.en: A rustic Slovenian loaf built mostly from buckwheat flour with just enough wheat flour for structure. Buckwheat thrives on poor mountain soil where wheat struggles, so this bread carried rural families through hard seasons for generations.
description.de: Ein rustikales slowenisches Brot, überwiegend aus Buchweizenmehl, mit gerade genug Weizenmehl für Struktur. Buchweizen gedeiht auf kargen Bergböden, wo Weizen versagt, weshalb dieses Brot Bauernfamilien über Generationen durch schwere Zeiten trug.
description.es: Un pan rústico esloveno hecho sobre todo con harina de alforfón, con apenas la harina de trigo necesaria para dar estructura. El alforfón prospera en suelos de montaña pobres donde el trigo falla, así que este pan sostuvo a familias rurales durante generaciones difíciles.
description.fr: Un pain rustique slovène composé surtout de farine de sarrasin, avec juste assez de farine de blé pour la structure. Le sarrasin prospère sur les sols pauvres de montagne où le blé peine, si bien que ce pain a nourri des familles rurales pendant des générations difficiles.

origin.note: Buckwheat suits Slovenia's poor mountain soils and short seasons better than wheat, making this loaf a historic staple of rural households; families reportedly kept baking it through wartime shortages.

ingredients.note: source says only "to taste"; a typical baker's-percentage estimate

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Combine buckwheat and wheat flours; make a well and dissolve the yeast in a little of the lukewarm water inside it.

method.title: Rest
method.summary: Dust the well with a little flour from the sides and let the yeast start working, about 10-15 minutes.

method.title: Knead
method.summary: Work in the remaining water, salt and oil and knead into a smooth, fairly stiff dough.

method.title: Bulk rise
method.summary: Cover and let rise in a warm spot until visibly puffed, about 45 minutes.

method.title: Shape
method.summary: Knock back gently and shape into an oval loaf on a baking tray.

method.title: Second rise
method.summary: Dust the top with flour and let rise again, about 30 minutes.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake at around 200°C for 45 minutes until the crust is deeply colored and crisp.

method.title: Cool
method.summary: Cool at least 10 minutes before the first slice; buckwheat's dense crumb firms up as it cools.

source.adaptation: Bake temperature/time (absent here) taken from a second recipe for the same bread. Yeast read as one 25g fresh-yeast cake; salt estimated since this source says only "to taste."
